title: Fix Owner Role Recognition Bug + Restore Clean Light Blue/White UI + Remove Logo

requirements.text: Fix the owner role authorization bug in the backend. All authorization checks that currently use `role === "admin"` must be changed to `role === "owner"`. The system must recognize OWNER001 as the owner and grant full super-admin access. Any middleware, guard, or access-control check that blocks owner operations (Add Worker, Edit Worker, Delete Worker, Change PIN, Modify Salary, Edit Attendance, and all protected APIs) must use the 'owner' role string exclusively. There must be no separate 'admin' role anywhere in the codebase.

quotes: Error: "Unauthorized: Only the owner can add workers"
Owner must have full access to: Add Worker, Edit Worker, Delete Worker, Change PIN, Modify Salary, Edit Attendance, Access all protected APIs
Remove any separate admin restriction. Owner = Super Admin.

acceptanceCriteria: Logging in as OWNER001 with PIN 1234 produces no 'Unauthorized' error
Owner can successfully add a new worker without any authorization error
Owner can edit, delete workers and change PINs without errors
No reference to role 'admin' exists in any authorization check
All protected backend calls succeed when called with the owner role

requirements.text: Fix the frontend login and auth context so that when OWNER001 logs in, the stored role is exactly 'owner' (not 'admin' or any other string), and all downstream role checks in the frontend use 'owner' as the owner role identifier consistently.

quotes: This is SINGLE OWNER SYSTEM. There is NO separate admin role.

acceptanceCriteria: After OWNER001 login, sessionStorage stores role='owner'
Owner Dashboard loads immediately after login with no redirect or auth error
All owner-only UI elements (Add Worker, Edit Worker, etc.) are visible and functional
No part of the frontend references or checks for role 'admin'

requirements.text: Restore a clean light blue and white visual theme across the entire app. Replace any dark blue gradients, heavy dark backgrounds, or dark-themed elements with: Header background using solid light blue (#E3F2FD to #1565C0 range, no dark gradient), page background white (#FFFFFF), cards white with a soft subtle box-shadow, clean spacing and modern minimal typography. Remove all dark gradient backgrounds from headers, sidebars, and navigation. The overall feel must be light, airy, and minimal.

quotes: Current UI dark blue heavy look aahe.
Header: Light Blue, Background: White, Cards: White with soft shadow, Clean spacing, Modern minimal UI, No dark gradient

acceptanceCriteria: App header uses a light blue color (no dark gradient)
All cards have white background with a subtle shadow
No dark gradient appears anywhere in the UI
Typography is clean and readable on white/light backgrounds
Layout remains responsive for mobile and desktop

requirements.text: Remove the image logo (and any logo icon, placeholder icon, or circular logo element) from the app header completely. The header must display only the plain text 'Aditi Electricals' — centered or left-aligned — with no image, no icon, no round circle, and no logo component of any kind.

quotes: Header should display ONLY: Aditi Electricals
No placeholder icon. No generated logo. No round logo circle. Just clean text title.

acceptanceCriteria: No image, icon, or SVG logo appears in the header
Header shows the text 'Aditi Electricals' as the sole brand identifier
Text is clean, readable, and styled consistently with the light blue theme
No broken image placeholders or empty logo containers are visible

constraints: Do NOT change attendance logic, salary logic, or notes logic
Do NOT change role separation between owner and worker
Do NOT change dashboard structure or page routing
Do NOT modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Changing the attendance time window or calendar color coding
Changing notes tabs or note type logic
Modifying PWA manifest or service worker logic
